title: Kaworu 2015 photo book out April
wikitext:
[]: Kaworu 2015 photo book out April             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sat Feb 07, 2015 11:10 pm Wonder if this'll have more content than that other Kaworu book? tweet-body — twitter-user (@username) date document.querySelectorAll('blockquote.twitter-tweet > a').forEach((t) => t.href = t.href.replace('x.com', 'twitter.com'))

[]:             Quote Post by Literary Eagle » Sun Feb 08, 2015 1:01 am Ooh, a new Evangelion book? Awesome! Must add this to my collection, heh. I'll keep an eye out for someplace to pre-order it. Thanks for posting about this!

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Sun Feb 08, 2015 5:55 am I know a friend who’d just love to have a book full of nothing but Kaworu centerfolds.

[]:             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Sun Feb 08, 2015 10:17 am FreakyFilmFan4ever wrote: I[s] know a friend who[/s]’d just love to have a book full of nothing but Kaworu centerfolds.

[]:             Quote Post by Shinoyami65 » Sun Feb 08, 2015 11:11 am A Kaworu photo book, eh?.... *Evil Laughter*

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Sun Feb 08, 2015 2:31 pm TMBounty_Hunter wrote: A friend huh?

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sun Feb 08, 2015 2:53 pm Freaky and TM : Nothing wrong with some Kaworu love! Literary Eagle wrote: Must add this to my collection, heh.

[]:             Quote Post by robersora » Mon Feb 09, 2015 4:47 am Oh oh oh, I hope to see some juicy stuff in there... Like an interview with Q - Kaworu about what he did for 15 years. Anyways, pretty cool stuff, I might get this one along with the one from 2007.

[]:             Quote Post by vito » Fri Feb 20, 2015 9:30 am Jacket http://animeanime.jp/article/2015/02/20/22057.html

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Fri Feb 20, 2015 9:37 am [Kaworu intensifies]

[]:             Quote Post by robersora » Fri Feb 20, 2015 11:41 am FreakyFilmFan4ever wrote: [Kaworu intensifies]

[]:             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Fri Feb 20, 2015 12:27 pm Nice theory, but it's immediately discredited by the fact that the screenshot on the site is actually 5 frame away from the one in your avatar Going by the googles it seems to be a collection of all the ENT related illustrations from 2007 onward so might not be any new material (other than the cover), especially since it's only 64 pages. But at least the fact that it's A4 and all color is nice. EDIT: Also comes with a B2 size poster.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Fri Feb 20, 2015 12:52 pm TMBounty_Hunter wrote: EDIT: Also comes with a B2 size poster.

[]:             Quote Post by Literary Eagle » Fri Feb 20, 2015 10:29 pm Ooh, I can't wait for April... Just take my money and give me the book now!

[]:             Quote Post by SawItAtAge10 » Tue Feb 24, 2015 7:25 pm Now here's something I would just LOVE to get my hands on!

[]:             Quote Post by Guy Nacks » Tue Feb 24, 2015 7:49 pm As Frank Costello once said: "Calm down or you'll shoot in your pants."

[]:             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Thu Apr 09, 2015 10:52 am http://neweva.blog103.fc2.com/blog-entry-3697.html From the photos it does look like it ended up being a compilation of previous promo art. Not even full page per pic? :( Is the cover pic really the only new one?

[]:             Quote Post by robersora » Thu Apr 09, 2015 11:00 am Meh, a little disappointing. He looks dashing on the cover art, though.

[]:             Quote Post by Literary Eagle » Fri Apr 10, 2015 11:37 pm I received my copy! (Wow, I was seriously not expecting this thing to arrive all the way from Japan so fast.) And yes, I did an illustrated review of it! I made this new post for it on my book review thread.
summary:
[]: Kaworu 2015 photo book out April             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sat Feb 07, 2015 11:10 pm Wonder if this'll have more content than that other Kaworu book? tweet-body — twitter-user (@username) date document.querySelectorAll('blockquote.twitter-tweet > a').forEach((t) => t.href = t.href.replace('x.com', 'twitter.com'))

[]:             Quote Post by Literary Eagle
